mnemonics: SELECT, PROJECT, JOIN - SPJ: The core operations of Relational Algebra, just like a powerful tool (SPJ) for your database.
keyPoints: Relational Algebra is a procedural query language that takes relations as input and returns relations as output.
Its core operations are Select (σ), Project (π), Join (⋈), Union (∪), Set Difference (-), and Cartesian Product (×).
It provides a theoretical foundation for SQL and query optimization.
commonMistakes: Confusing Select (σ) which chooses rows with Project (π) which chooses columns.
Forgetting that Set operations like Union require relations to be union-compatible (same number and type of attributes).
Using a theta-join (⋈θ) without specifying the join condition (θ).
